title: euphorbia serpillifolia var. neomexicana
intro: Euphorbia serpillifolia var. neomexicana, also known as the New Mexico Spurge or Spurred Euphorbia, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its annual growth habit and hairy stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can be used to treat various health conditions. Euphorbia serpillifolia var. neomexicana has been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and relief from coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations like infusions, salves, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested under proper guidance.
